poster_title: Know Your Limits: A Survey of Abstention in Large Language Models
authors: Bingbing Wen, Jihan Yao, Shangbin Feng, Chenjun Xu, Yulia Tsvetkov, Bill Howe, Lucy Lu Wang

content: LLMs show strong generalization but suffer from unreliability and hallucinations.
Abstention can enhance safety and reliability in LLM interactions.
Current abstention methods are limited and need improvement.
Calibration techniques are insufficient for query alignment with human values.
Need for robust abstention mechanisms across various tasks and contexts.
Current research lacks a comprehensive framework for abstention.
Framework for analyzing abstention from query answerability, model confidence, and human value alignment.
Categorization of abstention methods by model lifecycle stages.
Formal definition of the abstention function.
Survey of abstention methods and evaluation benchmarks.
Analysis of human value alignment using human annotations or proxy models.
Identifies gaps in current research and highlights the need for more nuanced abstention handling.
Provides a comprehensive overview of existing abstention methods and metrics.
Current abstention methods are primarily focused on LLMs as AI assistants.
The framework and methods are in early stages of development.
Underexplored aspects of abstention emphasize the need for further research.
Aims to broaden the scope and impact of abstention methodologies in AI systems.
Encourages development of robust and context-aware abstention mechanisms.
Integrates technical and ethical perspectives to enhance LLM trustworthiness.

Fig1.caption: Methods to improve LLM abstention grouped by pretraining, alignment, and inference stages.
Fig1.description: 展示提高大型语言模型 (LLM) 保留性能的方法，按预训练、对齐和推理阶段分类。
